title: Deep contrastive learning of molecular conformation for efficient property prediction
core_conclusion: LACL (Local Atomic environment Contrastive Learning) bridges the domain gap between expensive DFT-relaxed molecular geometries (source) and cheap MMFF/ML-generated conformations (target) via BGRL-based contrastive alignment, achieving quantum-chemical accuracy (<1 kcal/mol error) without DFT relaxation at inference. Validated on QM9 (134k molecules) and QMugs20 (68k drug-relevant conformations), LACL generalizes from small organics to long-chain pharmacological molecules and enables fast large-scale molecular property screening.
why_it_matters: Bypasses the cubic-scaling DFT relaxation bottleneck enabling high-throughput screening; produces domain-agnostic latent space capturing local atomic environment semantics rather than geometric artifacts; plugs directly into inverse molecular design and active learning loops; generalizes to biological and pharmacological long-chain molecules.
why_nature: Addresses a universal bottleneck in computational chemistry affecting any GNN trained on DFT data; introduces principled equivariant and contrastive architecture with dual-benchmark validation; dual-benchmark validation on QM9 and QMugs provides strong generality evidence; full open science release on GitHub and figshare meets NCS reproducibility standards.
added_value_to_my_research: LACL domain-adaptation strategy -> contrastive alignment of expensive survey-measured accessibility (source) vs. cheap OSM-proximity proxy (target) for data-scarce city generalization in 2SFCA/R2SFCA models
Equivariant latent-vector GNN layer -> physics-informed spatial graph operator encoding street-network geometry constraints for more data-efficient spatiotemporal health-outcome GNNs

Multi-conformer ensemble extension of LACL -> set-level contrastive encoder aligning mobile-phone, social-media, and remote-sensing views of the same urban phenomenon for robust multi-source data fusion
LACL as fast property oracle in active learning -> LACL-inspired cheap accessibility oracle accelerating combinatorial facility-location search for equitable EV charging and healthcare siting
LACL latent-space explainability via attribution methods -> audit domain-adapted GeoAI models for interpretable neighborhood-feature attribution in spatially-varying-coefficient and MGWR models

related.title: Molecular contrastive learning of representations via graph neural networks
related.relation: foundational molecular contrastive learning approach (MolCLR) that LACL extends to the domain-adaptation problem

related.title: Traversing chemical space with active deep learning for low-data drug discovery
related.relation: active learning oracle loop that LACL's fast inference could accelerate for drug candidate screening

related.title: Molecular set representation learning
related.relation: set-level encoder architecture for aggregating multi-conformer molecular ensembles, extending LACL's pairwise contrastive loss

related.title: Overcoming the barrier of orbital-free density functional theory for molecular systems using deep learning
related.relation: deep learning replacement for expensive quantum-chemistry computation, complementary to LACL's geometry-level domain bypass

related.title: Navigating molecular OOD-ness
related.relation: out-of-distribution detection and generalization for molecular ML, directly relevant to auditing LACL's domain-adaptation fidelity

figures.caption: Comparison of previous DFT-constrained molecular property prediction approach vs. LACL — illustrates the domain-shift problem in the encoded space and how contrastive alignment achieves domain-agnostic representations and improved parity-plot accuracy

Overall LACL architecture — (a) dual-view contrastive alignment pipeline; (b) modified ALIGNN encoder with bond-angle line graph; (c) sequential graph convolutions; (d) equivariant latent-vector update rule via outer product of phi_ij and unit vector u_ij
